ref: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
text: Nya ófa bembembe ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Onanto Fanlonlo eto Ubi! Be nkpe lǝ mɔ ku fǝ lenti mfo? Nlɛ fǝ lekpakpa, se mɛ uyi lǝ Onanto eto diye ǝsuǝ fǝnkǝ embunǝ mɛ koto!”
translated: The child then asked, “Onanto Fanlonlo and Ubi! Why do you keep coming to my house? If you come again, I will tell Onanto that you are disturbing me!”

ref: “An enemy did this,” he replied. His servants then asked, “Do you want us to go out and pull up the weeds?”
text: “Útǝ mǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, ‘Olontidi utsyuǝ díyifo nyamfo nɛ.’ “Bátɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, ‘Ala fǝ busu busu sefa siamǝ bulǝkǝ?’
translated: When the child was about to eat, the elder said, 'Olontidi, don't eat the food.' The child asked, 'Why do you keep food and not let us eat it?'

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Se Oka Herodes díkpǝ eto kamaa, Onanto eto ɔtɔnkpe utsyuǝ ǝlǝkǝ ǝsuǝ óte Yosef lǝ kola kǝmiǝ lǝ Egipte.
translated: When King Herod died, an angel appeared to Joseph in a dream and told him to return from Egypt.

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Bibu bebe kase dituwinimi lǝ dimǝ laanɔ bobee. Kaanta olokotutu nya keendi kafia kefi keti ǝsuǝ.
translated: The child is not happy because of the way the other children are treating him. He has decided to go home and tell his parents everything.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Mimba lɛbɔ bitsyi lǝ bawa eto kafo ǝsuǝ! Asa sɛɛ aambɔ ketsyi lǝ mi kɛnyɛ! Itsyise lesa nǝ diyi mi ketu ǝsuǝǝ, nnya mi kɛnyɛ lǝǝlǝkǝ katɛyi nɛ.
translated: The child is not as strong as the father, but he is wiser! If you don't know something, ask those who know. Even if you are older, you can still learn from the young, and that is not a shame.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: “Nyaso lǝ utsyuǝ átɛyi mi nkǝ, ‘Bebe! Nnyǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ nkpe mfo o-o,’ nye ‘Nnyǝ wǝ nkpe mmǝǝ,’ mamfo wǝ anɔ.
translated: The child said to the mother, 'Mother! I have seen Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo's child,' and she said, 'I have seen the child too,' the mother replied.

ref: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
text: Nya Petro lɛtɛyi nkǝ, “Ekpe lǝ lesa saa bǝ ɔnukualɛ minlɛ bɔtɛyi minkǝ Onanto lǝ unǝ mɛ koto lǝ sidia minlɛ bɔtɛyi!” Lefe nǝmfo tutuutu nnya ukusɛ dítǝ kɛnyɛ.
translated: Petro said to them, "If Ekpe comes to the village, he will not find any food, and if Onanto comes, he will not find any water!" The people were very afraid and ran away.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
text: Nyaso Farisifɔ bǝtsyuǝ átɔ Yesu bǝnkǝ, “Beso fǝ bakasebi lǝǝyifo nko? Bo kɔfi mǝntǝ kusu bǝ beyifo nko lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi.”
translated: When the Pharisees saw that Jesus was eating with tax collectors and sinners, they asked his disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?”

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Itǝ esoo, Yesu eto diye áyɛ letsyo lǝ Galilea eto kasɔ kenke. Yesu Átsya Betidi Kpǝ Bufi.
translated: At that time, Jesus went to a town in Galilee called Cana.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Asa nyamfo eyifo asa nnya bamamonyǝ lǝǝsiǝ kebu disi ketsyiko. Mi Anto wǝ nkpe kato yi nkǝ asa nyamfo kenke ebohiã mi.
translated: The child who is not obedient will not be able to eat the food that is prepared. So Anto should listen and be obedient to his parents.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Mimba ni babuayifo kulaa yi kase buifi asa sɛɛ bitǝ mi bebi. Nya ntsyǝ yuutsyǝ ku Boanto wǝ nkpe kato itǝ bo? Ntsyǝ yoofo bubǝ bǝ weembufi wǝ asa, asa sɛɛ, utǝ betidi ba laatɔ wǝ?
translated: The child is crying because he wants to eat but there is no food in the house. Mother, why did you leave Boanto and come here? You said you would bring food, food, where is it now?

ref: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
text: Se bánɔ nyamfo, íyifo mǝ yanii nya bédu wǝ lǝ ɔflɔ. Yesu Áte Nkǝ Bekpǝnkpǝ Si Bootaka.
translated: If you love me, keep my commandments. Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
